text:
SAINT QUENTIN-EN-YVELINES, France 芒聙聰 Viktor Hovland held his palms up while searching for his golf ball in the rough beside the 14th fairway at Le Golf National, his expression summing up his frustration.

Jon Rahm and Xander Schauffele were seen shuffling their feet in the tall grass, as were caddies, officials, volunteers and Hovland, trying without much luck to find Hovland芒聙聶s golf ball. Just as Hovland was prepared to give up, the ball was found by a tournament volunteer.

He ended up making a par on the hole but by then the damage was long done.

A triple-bogey 7 at the first where he badly pulled an iron shot off the tee and into the water芒聙聰and then hit his third in the water芒聙聰led to a bad day for Hovland, who shot 75 and is outside of the top 50 in a 60-player tournament.

It continued a perplexing year for the reigning FedEx Cup champion, who looked nearly unbeatable late last year but has struggled throughout 2024.

芒聙聹I芒聙聶m just playing poorly,芒聙聶芒聙聶 Hovland said afterward. 芒聙聹The ball is not going where I want it to. And out here you芒聙聶re going to be punished.芒聙聶芒聙聶

Hovland芒聙聶s decline this year has been one of the top stories in the game. After contending in major championships over each of the past two years and storming to that FedEx Cup title, the Norwegian seemed poised to take a step up this year.

But aside from a third-place finish at the PGA Championship, where he found form after recently having gotten back with his coach, Joe Mayo, the results have again been poor.

Hovland missed the cut in three of the four major championships this year.脗聽 The third-place finish at Valhalla is his only top-10 and his best outside of that is a tie for 15th at the Memorial, where he won a year earlier.

芒聙聹I芒聙聶m still capable of playing really good golf despite some really big technical issues at the moment,芒聙聶芒聙聶 Hovland said. 芒聙聹And I think the PGA was a sign of that. But if the machine is broken I can芒聙聶t really overcome that. And right now, I芒聙聶ve got to fix the machine.芒聙聶芒聙聶

In comments after the round to reporter Espen Blaker of Eurosport/Discovery made in Norwegian, Hovland said: 芒聙聹I'm not playing well at all. My game feels very unstable, and there is way too much random stuff happening out there.

芒聙聹I got a big slap in the face on the opening hole, and couldn't recover from there. My game is just not good enough at the moment, that is just the facts.芒聙聶芒聙聶

Hovland said playing with Schauffele and Rahm only extenuated the differences at the moment.

芒聙聹There is a big contrast, that芒聙聶s for sure,芒聙聶芒聙聶 he said. 芒聙聹The differences are quite massive at the moment. I know I have it in me to play at the same level, but right now it feels quite far away.芒聙聶芒聙聶

Schauffele tried to give the situation some perspective.

芒聙聹It芒聙聶s just golf; he芒聙聶ll figure it out,芒聙聶芒聙聶 he said. 芒聙聹He's way too stoic, I guess, and too good at golf. I've played with Hovi; all of us have played worse or better. I've played with him at his best. It's special.

芒聙聹So now is now, and in two or three months, or hopefully in the next couple of weeks for him, he'll figure it out. But I don't think anybody is going to worry about him.芒聙聶芒聙聶

The former Oklahoma State golfer said he went 芒聙聹down a few rabbit holes芒聙聶芒聙聶 in search of his game and told Sirius XM radio at the PGA he had considered withdrawing from the tournament.

芒聙聹I wasn芒聙聶t playing good at all,芒聙聶芒聙聶 he said. 芒聙聹Overall, I thought things didn芒聙聶t feel very good, and when they don芒聙聶t feel good, it芒聙聶s like 芒聙聹What芒聙聶s the point of playing.芒聙聶芒聙聶

But play he did andhe came down the stretch with a chancebefore falling short of Schauffele and runner-up Bryson DeChambeau. It seemed like a turning point, especially since he had returned to Mayo and found some immediate success.

At the PGA, Hovland said that Mayo helped identify a feel he wanted to recreate and the two worked on it together in the days leading up to the first round. Even Hovland was surprised how quickly it took.

But since then he tied for 15th脗聽at the Memorial, then a missed cut at the U.S. Open, tied for 20th脗聽at the Travelers, tied for 46th脗聽at the Scottish Open and a missed cut at the British Open.

芒聙聹I felt I saw some instant improvements when I went back to Joe Mayo, but since then it's been hard to build on that and make the improvements we are looking for,芒聙聶芒聙聶 he said. 芒聙聹It is frustrating... I don't really know what to say. Golf isn't very easy at the moment.芒聙聶芒聙聶

Hovland said not having Mayo with him at Le Golf National has made working through his issues a bit more challenging. He芒聙聶s got two more rounds to play here, then a week off prior to the FedEx Cup playoffs.

At 55th脗聽in the standings, Hovland is not assured of advancing to the BMW Championship (top 50 ) and Tour Championship (top 30), the tournaments he won last year.

芒聙聹I芒聙聶m not going to be able to do a whole lot the next couple of rounds,芒聙聶芒聙聶 he said. 芒聙聹I芒聙聶m just going to make the most out of it and get back to work.芒聙聶芒聙聶

Bob Harig is a senior writer covering golf for Sports Illustrated. He has more than 25 years experience on the beat, including 15 at ESPN. Harig is a regular guest on Sirius XM PGA Tour Radio and has written two books, 芒聙聹DRIVE: The Lasting Legacy of Tiger Woods芒聙聺 and 芒聙聹Tiger and Phil: Golf芒聙聶s Most Fascinating Rivalry.芒聙聺 He graduated from Indiana University where he earned an Evans Scholarship, named in honor of the great amateur golfer Charles (Chick) Evans Jr. Harig, a former president of the Golf Writers Association of America, lives in Clearwater, Fla.

title: Viktor Hovland's Long, Mystifying Season Continues at Paris Olympics
description: Hovland won the FedEx Cup in 2023 and seemed poised for a breakout this year. But he's currently near the bottom of the Olympic standings through 36 holes, continuing a season of perplexing results.
